_description: NHEFS + NHANES I wave mapping. NHEFS is a 5-wave longitudinal study: the NHANES I baseline examination (1971-75) plus four follow-up waves (1982-84, 1986, 1987, 1992). Each wave has its own eligibility rules and target sample size — see per-wave 'eligible_n' and 'interviewed_n' fields. Unlike HRS (biennial and synchronous), NHEFS wave spacing is irregular and the 1986 wave was restricted to subjects 55-74 at baseline.
_cohort_definition: All persons 25-74 years of age who completed a medical examination at NHANES I in 1971-75

notes: NHANES I examined ~23,808 people across all ages; the NHEFS cohort is the 14,407 of those who were 25-74 at exam.

The cardio/respiratory supplement (DU4091) was given to only a subsample of ~6,913 subjects. Analyses requiring variables from DU4091 (e.g., baseline smoking) will lose roughly half the NHEFS cohort on merge.

Demographics (age, sex, race, education) are repeated in each DU#### file under that module's variable prefix (N1BM#### for DU4111, N1AH#### for DU4081, etc.). Prefer NHEFS vitl for demographics to avoid prefix mismatch.

Conducted in person. The interview file has 36 SEQNUM variants (SEQNUM1..SEQNUM36), one header per interview section. Use SEQNUM1 as the canonical merge key — all variants carry the same subject ID per row.
This wave includes physical measurements (weight, pulse, blood pressure) taken in person. Later waves (1986, 1987, 1992) are telephone-only with no physical measures.
Also includes a recall of baseline smoking status, which is the preferred source for 'smoked at baseline' variables (better coverage than DU4091 baseline supplement).
RESTRICTED to subjects 55-74 at baseline and not known to be deceased at the 1982-84 NHEFS. Only ~3,980 subjects eligible.
Telephone interview only — no physical measurements.
Not used in Phase 1 (Phase 1 uses baseline + 1982 only). Listed here for Phase 2 scope.

CUMULATIVE files: vitl.txt covers the full 14,407 cohort (vital + tracing status across all prior waves), and mort.txt covers all 4,497 decedents identified through 1992. These two files anchor the whole cohort regardless of which follow-up wave the analysis targets.
Phase 1 uses n92vitl.txt (demographics, vital status) and N92mort.txt (mortality) but does NOT use the 1992 interview file.

phase_1_notes: Phase 1 scope: baseline (for pre-quit covariates) + 1982 interview (for 1982 weight and smoking-quit status) + 1992 vital/mortality (for optional bonus survival analyses).
The 1986 and 1987 waves are intentionally excluded from Phase 1 to keep scope tight.
